paper_id: Fischer-Pauzenberger and Schwaiger - 2017 - The OntoREA Accounting and Finance Model Ontological Conceptualization of the Accounting and Finan
input_limitations: Figures are referenced but not visible (shown as [FIGURE] placeholders); some OCR artifacts (e.g., 'fi' ligatures, spacing irregularities) in the extracted text.

evidence_quotes.quote: In this article ontological meta-properties of OntoUML are used to specify these peculiarities and to derive the OntoREA © Accounting and Finance Model , which constitutes a valid ontology-based conceptualization of the accounting and fi nance domain.
This model should be bene fi cial especially for business analysts who have to understand and develop conceptual models for up-to-date enterprise and accounting information systems.
The deeper integration of the fi nance into the accounting domain by using the OntoUML language for the ontological conceptualization constitutes the primary research objective of this article.

goals.justification: The model is explicitly framed as an ontology-based conceptualization that clarifies and defines concepts (derivative instruments, balanced reciprocity, ALE) in the accounting and finance domain, matching the 'conceptualize' and 'understand' signals for For Understanding.

evidence_quotes.quote: Such systems could be designed and implemented e.g. within a model-driven software development approach (see e.g. Brambilla et al. [28], Stahl and V ö lter [29] or Rybola et al. [30]) by establishing the OntoREA © model fi rst and subsequently transforming it into a software application.

The resulting model can be used for different purposes. One possibility is its usage in the accounting (AIS -see Steinbart and Romney [26]) as well as enterprise information systems (EIS -see Dunn et al. [27]) research by establishing ontology-based accounting and enterprise information systems.

goals.justification: The authors explicitly frame the model's intended use as a foundation for building accounting/enterprise information systems via model-driven software development (transforming the model into a software application), matching the 'used to create' / artifact-generation signal. Coded Medium because the implementation is stated as an intended use ('could be') rather than already executed.

evidence_quotes.quote: Depending on the purpose the derivate instruments can be shown on a net basis for fi nancial reporting purposes or on an un-consolidated basis for strategic and management control purposes.
evidence_quotes.section: 4 OntoREA © Accounting and Finance Model: Collectives Beyond ALE Phases

evidence_quotes.quote: Schwaiger identi fi ed several de fi ciencies concerning the traditional Asset-Liability-Equity (ALE) accounting logic [12].

uncertain_goals.reason: Mentions of flexibility for management control and identifying deficiencies are incidental and not framed as the model's primary purpose; the disambiguation rule says when evidence shows implementation/conceptualization rather than separate decision-making support, do not double-code For Problem-solving.

evidence_quotes.quote: This model should be bene fi cial especially for business analysts who have to understand and develop conceptual models for up-to-date enterprise and accounting information systems.

uncertain_goals.reason: The reference to business analysts developing conceptual models could weakly imply modeling-method learning, but the surrounding context targets domain understanding rather than learning the OntoUML method itself, so the evidence is weak.
